annotations.label: Gamma distribution

anchor.snippet: the gamma distribution is a versatile two- parameter family of continuous probability distributions

annotations.note: `gammaMeasure a r` defines the gamma distribution on ℝ as `volume.withDensity (gammaPDF a r)` with shape `a` and rate `r`.

annotations.label: Special cases of gamma

anchor.snippet: The exponential distribution , Erlang distribution , and chi-squared distribution are special cases of the gamma distribution

annotations.note: Only the exponential special case is formalized: `expMeasure r := gammaMeasure 1 r`; Erlang and chi-squared distributions are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximum entropy characterization

anchor.snippet: The gamma distribution is the maximum entropy probability distribution

annotations.note: No max-entropy characterization of the gamma distribution is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Erlang as sum of exponentials

anchor.snippet: If α is a positive integer , then the distribution represents an Erlang distribution

annotations.note: The Erlang distribution and its representation as a sum of independent exponentials are not formalized.

annotations.label: Shape-rate parameterization

anchor.section: Characterization using shape α and rate β
anchor.snippet: The gamma distribution can be parameterized in terms of a shape parameter α and an inverse scale parameter

annotations.note: `gammaMeasure a r` is the shape-rate parameterization (parameter `r` is rate).

annotations.label: PDF (shape-rate)

anchor.section: Characterization using shape α and rate β
anchor.snippet: The corresponding probability density function in the shape-rate parameterization is

annotations.note: `gammaPDFReal a r x = r^a / Gamma a * x^(a-1) * exp (-(r*x))` for `0 ≤ x` matches the shape-rate PDF.

annotations.label: CDF as regularized gamma (rate)

anchor.section: Characterization using shape α and rate β
anchor.snippet: The cumulative distribution function is the regularized gamma function:

annotations.note: The gamma CDF is expressed as an integral of the PDF, but Mathlib has no regularized lower incomplete gamma function to equate it to.

annotations.label: Erlang CDF series expansion

anchor.section: Characterization using shape α and rate β
anchor.snippet: the cumulative distribution function has the following series expansion

annotations.note: The Erlang CDF series expansion is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shape-scale parameterization

anchor.section: Characterization using shape α and scale θ
anchor.snippet: A random variable X that is gamma-distributed with shape α and scale θ is denoted

annotations.note: Mathlib parameterizes the gamma by shape and rate; the shape-scale form (θ = 1/r) is not separately defined but follows by substitution.

annotations.label: PDF (shape-scale)

anchor.section: Characterization using shape α and scale θ
anchor.snippet: The probability density function using the shape-scale parametrization is

annotations.note: Only the shape-rate form `gammaPDFReal` is defined; shape-scale would require substituting `r = 1/θ`.

annotations.label: CDF as regularized gamma (scale)

anchor.section: Characterization using shape α and scale θ
anchor.snippet: The cumulative distribution function is the regularized gamma function:

annotations.note: Mathlib has the CDF as an integral but no regularized incomplete gamma function and no explicit shape-scale form.

annotations.label: Erlang CDF (scale form)

anchor.section: Characterization using shape α and scale θ
anchor.snippet: It can also be expressed as follows, if α is a positive integer

annotations.note: Erlang-form CDF is not formalized.

annotations.label: Mean of gamma

anchor.snippet: The mean of gamma distribution is given by the product of its shape and scale parameters

annotations.note: The mean of the gamma distribution is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Chen–Rubin median bounds

anchor.snippet: A rigorous treatment of the problem of determining an asymptotic expansion and bounds for the median of the gamma distribution was handled first by Chen and Rubin

annotations.note: Chen–Rubin median bounds are not in Mathlib.
